title: Soy candle — apple and cinnamon
subtitle: A warm autumn soy wax container candle with apple and cinnamon fragrance oil
excerpt: Apple and cinnamon fragrance oil is a classic autumn and winter household scent, spicy and sweet in equal measure. This soy wax container candle holds the fragrance well and fills a medium room with scent within 30 minutes of lighting. The method is straightforward: melt, cool, add fragrance, pour.
sourceNotes: Soy wax container candle method: Bramble Berry candle-making guide (2023). Apple and cinnamon fragrance oil: spicy fragrance family; check flash point before use (most are above 65°C). Fragrance load 7% of wax weight. Pour temperature 55 to 60°C.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 candle (200 ml glass jar, ~150 g finished wax)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale wax and fragrance proportionally for multiple jars.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cure for at least 3 days before the first burn.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Two years away from direct sunlight. Fragrance fades after around two years; wax is stable indefinitely.

glossaryTerms.definition: The percentage of fragrance oil by wax weight. At 7%, the scent throw is strong and even without the fragrance pooling.
The first burn. Allow the melt pool to reach the jar edge to set the candle's burn pattern.
When a candle burns down the centre without melting the edges. Caused by extinguishing before the first melt pool reaches the jar edge.

content.text: Apple and cinnamon is one of the most reliable seasonal fragrance oils in a soy wax container candle. At 7%

, it gives a warm, spiced scent in a medium room. The apple and cinnamon family can sometimes cause slight discolouration to the wax (a light caramel tone); this is cosmetic only and does not affect burn performance. Pour at 55 to 60°C and cure for at least 3 days.
Melt wax over a simmering-water bath on low heat only. Never leave melting wax unattended. Keep a kitchen-fire blanket within reach.

items.prepNote: 7% of wax weight; check flash point is above 65°C before use
Pre-tabbed cotton wick for a 65 mm jar

content.text: Wick, melt, add fragrance, and pour

Centre the pre-tabbed wick in the jar. Melt soy wax in the candle pitcher over a simmering-water bath to 65°C. Remove from heat. When cooled to 55 to 60°C, add the fragrance oil and stir for 2 minutes. Pour slowly into the jar, leaving 10 mm headspace. Tap once to release air bubbles.
Leave undisturbed for 3 to 5 days. Trim the wick to 5 mm before the first burn.
. Allow the melt pool to reach the jar edge before extinguishing (3 to 4 hours). Extinguishing early causes
Two years away from direct sunlight. Store with a lid or cover.
